steps.title: Use total sums, not averages alone
steps.content:
The total of all $175$ boiling points is:
$$175\cdot167$$
The total for the $50$ semivolatile compounds is:
$$50\cdot322$$

steps.title: Subtract to get the volatile total
steps.content:
The remaining $125$ compounds are volatile, so their total boiling point is:
$$175\cdot167-50\cdot322=13,125$$

steps.title: Divide by the 125 volatile compounds
steps.content:
Mean equals total divided by count:
$$\frac{13,125}{125}=105$$
The mean boiling point of the volatile compounds is 105 degrees Celsius.
